pending.body:
finding_summary: Daemon failure reconciliation can bind a same-run retry to the previous failed attempt when the retry's `BeadStarted` lineage write is missing. line_refs: [src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:171-217, src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2335-2405]. severity: high. affected_files: [src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs]. remediation_hint: Do not fall back from the current retry attempt to an older terminal lineage row with the same `run_id`; require an exact/non-terminal lineage match, or synthesize the missing retry start row before calling `reconcile_failure()`, otherwise fail closed to `NeedsOperator`.

`lineage_completion_attempt_identity()` currently falls back to the newest row with the same `run_id` even when that row is an older terminal failure. `try_reconcile_failure()` then uses that stale `started_at` for `reconcile_failure()`. In the partial-write case this feature is meant to recover from, the new failed retry is recorded as the old attempt again, which undercounts retries and repairs the original failure row/event in place instead of preserving a distinct later failure.

finding_summary: CLI milestone sync still cannot reconcile a failed retry that reused the same `run_id` when the retry's start lineage was missed. line_refs: [src/cli/run.rs:1203-1247, src/cli/run.rs:1320-1345, src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:391-406]. severity: medium. affected_files: [src/cli/run.rs, src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs]. remediation_hint: Mirror the existing success-path repair before calling `reconcile_failure_sync()`: when the project journal/snapshot prove a newer same-run retry but milestone lineage only has the older terminal row, synthesize or repair the retry start row first.

`sync_terminal_milestone_task_with_options()` skips its missing-lineage repair whenever `same_named_terminal_attempt_exists` is true. That is exactly the state you get after a prior failure on the same `run_id`. The failure branch then calls `reconcile_failure_sync()` with the new attempt's `started_at`, but `record_bead_completion_with_disposition()` still requires an exact lineage row, so sync errors out instead of counting attempt 2/3 and leaving the bead retryable.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 19 -> Plan and Implement
